heading: 9.7.14.6.Matrix multiply-accumulate operation usingmma.spinstruction with sparse matrix A (part 5)
instruction: Matrix multiply-accumulate operation usingmma.spinstruction with sparse matrix A
content:
> 

[Figure: sparse-mma-metadata-168128-u4s4-first64col]
SPARSE MMA METADATA LAYOUT DESCRIPTION
========================================

1. MMA SHAPE AND DATA TYPES
----------------------------
Shape: .m16n8k128
Data types: .u4 (unsigned 4-bit), .s4 (signed 4-bit), .e2m1 (2-bit exponent, 1-bit mantissa)
This figure covers columns 0 through 63 (the first half of the k=128 dimension).

2. TABLE STRUCTURE
------------------
The table has rows 0–15 (16 rows total, corresponding to the m=16 dimension) and 8 column groups:
  Columns 0..7
  Columns 8..15
  Columns 16..23
  Columns 24..31
  Columns 32..39
  Columns 40..47
  Columns 48..55
  Columns 56..63

Rows 2–6 and rows 10–14 are omitted with "..." notation, indicating the same pattern continues.

3. THREAD ASSIGNMENTS AND BIT POSITIONS
-----------------------------------------
The notation is: T<thread_index>: [high_bit..low_bit]

The thread index uses a parameterized variable "i" where i ranges to cover all 32 threads in a warp.

ROW 0:
  Columns 0..7:    T_{2i}:     [3..0]
  Columns 8..15:   T_{2i}:     [7..4]
  Columns 16..23:  T_{2i}:     [11..8]
  Columns 24..31:  T_{2i}:     [15..12]
  Columns 32..39:  T_{2i}:     [19..16]
  Columns 40..47:  T_{2i}:     [23..20]
  Columns 48..55:  T_{2i}:     [27..24]
  Columns 56..63:  T_{2i}:     [31..28]

ROW 1:
  Columns 0..7:    T_{2i+4}:   [3..0]
  Columns 8..15:   T_{2i+4}:   [7..4]
  Columns 16..23:  T_{2i+4}:   [11..8]
  Columns 24..31:  T_{2i+4}:   [15..12]
  Columns 32..39:  T_{2i+4}:   [19..16]
  Columns 40..47:  T_{2i+4}:   [23..20]
  Columns 48..55:  T_{2i+4}:   [27..24]
  Columns 56..63:  T_{2i+4}:   [31..28]

ROW 7:
  Columns 0..7:    T_{2i+28}:  [3..0]
  Columns 8..15:   T_{2i+28}:  [7..4]
  Columns 16..23:  T_{2i+28}:  [11..8]
  Columns 24..31:  T_{2i+28}:  [15..12]
  Columns 32..39:  T_{2i+28}:  [19..16]
  Columns 40..47:  T_{2i+28}:  [23..20]
  Columns 48..55:  T_{2i+28}:  [27..24]
  Columns 56..63:  T_{2i+28}:  [31..28]

ROW 8:
  Columns 0..7:    T_{2i+1}:   [3..0]
  Columns 8..15:   T_{2i+1}:   [7..4]
  Columns 16..23:  T_{2i+1}:   [11..8]
  Columns 24..31:  T_{2i+1}:   [15..12]
  Columns 32..39:  T_{2i+1}:   [19..16]
  Columns 40..47:  T_{2i+1}:   [23..20]
  Columns 48..55:  T_{2i+1}:   [27..24]
  Columns 56..63:  T_{2i+1}:   [31..28]

ROW 9:
  Columns 0..7:    T_{2i+5}:   [3..0]
  Columns 8..15:   T_{2i+5}:   [7..4]
  Columns 16..23:  T_{2i+5}:   [11..8]
  Columns 24..31:  T_{2i+5}:   [15..12]
  Columns 32..39:  T_{2i+5}:   [19..16]
  Columns 40..47:  T_{2i+5}:   [23..20]
  Columns 48..55:  T_{2i+5}:   [27..24]
  Columns 56..63:  T_{2i+5}:   [31..28]

ROW 15:
  Columns 0..7:    T_{2i+29}:  [3..0]
  Columns 8..15:   T_{2i+29}:  [7..4]
  Columns 16..23:  T_{2i+29}:  [11..8]
  Columns 24..31:  T_{2i+29}:  [15..12]
  Columns 32..39:  T_{2i+29}:  [19..16]
  Columns 40..47:  T_{2i+29}:  [23..20]
  Columns 48..55:  T_{2i+29}:  [27..24]
  Columns 56..63:  T_{2i+29}:  [31..28]

4. METADATA ENCODING FORMAT
-----------------------------
Each thread holds a 32-bit metadata register. The 32 bits are divided into eight 4-bit fields:
  Bits [3..0]   -> metadata for columns 0..7
  Bits [7..4]   -> metadata for columns 8..15
  Bits [11..8]  -> metadata for columns 16..23
  Bits [15..12] -> metadata for columns 24..31
  Bits [19..16] -> metadata for columns 32..39
  Bits [23..20] -> metadata for columns 40..47
  Bits [27..24] -> metadata for columns 48..55
  Bits [31..28] -> metadata for columns 56..63

Each 4-bit field encodes the sparsity selection for 8 consecutive columns (one group of 8 elements). For 2:4 structured sparsity at 4-bit (u4/s4) or e2m1 precision, each group of 4 elements has 2 non-zero values selected by 2-bit indices, so 4 bits cover 2 selections of 2 bits each within the 8-column group.

5. THREAD GROUPING AND ROW PATTERNS
--------------------------------------
Rows 0–7 (upper half of m=16):
  Row 0 uses thread T_{2i}
  Row 1 uses thread T_{2i+4}
  Row 2 uses thread T_{2i+8}   (implied by pattern)
  Row 3 uses thread T_{2i+12}  (implied)
  Row 4 uses thread T_{2i+16}  (implied)
  Row 5 uses thread T_{2i+20}  (implied)
  Row 6 uses thread T_{2i+24}  (implied)
  Row 7 uses thread T_{2i+28}
  -> Thread offset increments by +4 per row for rows 0–7, using even-indexed base threads (T_{2i})

Rows 8–15 (lower half of m=16):
  Row 8  uses thread T_{2i+1}
  Row 9  uses thread T_{2i+5}
  Row 10 uses thread T_{2i+9}  (implied)
  Row 11 uses thread T_{2i+13} (implied)
  Row 12 uses thread T_{2i+17} (implied)
  Row 13 uses thread T_{2i+21} (implied)
  Row 14 uses thread T_{2i+25} (implied)
  Row 15 uses thread T_{2i+29}
  -> Thread offset increments by +4 per row for rows 8–15, using odd-indexed base threads (T_{2i+1})

SUMMARY OF KEY PATTERNS:
- Each thread holds exactly 32 bits of metadata covering all 8 column groups (0..63) for a single row.
- The 32-bit register is fully consumed by the 8 column groups (8 groups x 4 bits = 32 bits).
- Rows 0–7 are handled by even-offset threads (2i, 2i+4, ..., 2i+28).
- Rows 8–15 are handled by odd-offset threads (2i+1, 2i+5, ..., 2i+29).
- The parameter i indexes pairs of threads, stepping through the warp in groups of 2.
- This figure covers only columns 0–63; a companion figure covers columns 64–127 for the full k=128 extent.

  >
  >
  > Figure 146 Sparse MMA .m16n8k128 metadata layout for columns 0–63 for `.u4`/`.s4`/`.e2m1` type.
  >
  >
  > 

[Figure: sparse-mma-metadata-168128-u4s4-last64col]
SPARSE MMA METADATA LAYOUT DESCRIPTION
Figure: Sparse MMA .m16n8k128 metadata layout for columns 64-127 for .u4/.s4/.e2m1 type

==============================================================
1. MMA SHAPE AND DATA TYPES
==============================================================
MMA Shape: m16n8k128
Data Types: .u4 (unsigned 4-bit integer), .s4 (signed 4-bit integer), .e2m1 (2-bit exponent, 1-bit mantissa floating point)
Column range covered by this figure: columns 64 through 127
This figure is the second half of the full k=128 metadata layout (columns 0-63 are in a companion figure)

==============================================================
2. TABLE STRUCTURE
==============================================================
Rows: 0, 1, 2, ..., 7, 8, 9, 10, ..., 15 (16 rows total, representing the M=16 dimension)
Columns: 64..71, 72..79, 80..87, 88..95, 96..103, 104..111, 112..119, 120..127 (8 column groups of 8 columns each, covering K=64 through K=127)

==============================================================
3. COMPLETE THREAD AND BIT ASSIGNMENTS (ALL CELLS)
==============================================================

ROW 0:
  Columns 64..71:   T2:  [3..0]
  Columns 72..79:   T2:  [7..4]
  Columns 80..87:   T2:  [11..8]
  Columns 88..95:   T2:  [15..12]
  Columns 96..103:  T2:  [19..16]
  Columns 104..111: T2:  [23..20]
  Columns 112..119: T2:  [27..24]
  Columns 120..127: T2:  [31..28]

ROW 1:
  Columns 64..71:   T6:  [3..0]
  Columns 72..79:   T6:  [7..4]
  Columns 80..87:   T6:  [11..8]
  Columns 88..95:   T6:  [15..12]
  Columns 96..103:  T6:  [19..16]
  Columns 104..111: T6:  [23..20]
  Columns 112..119: T6:  [27..24]
  Columns 120..127: T6:  [31..28]

ROW 2:
  (cells are empty/blank in the figure — implied continuation of pattern)

ROWS 3..6: (indicated by "..." — blank/implied continuation)

ROW 7:
  Columns 64..71:   T30: [3..0]
  Columns 72..79:   T30: [7..4]
  Columns 80..87:   T30: [11..8]
  Columns 88..95:   T30: [15..12]
  Columns 96..103:  T30: [19..16]
  Columns 104..111: T30: [23..20]
  Columns 112..119: T30: [27..24]
  Columns 120..127: T30: [31..28]

ROW 8:
  Columns 64..71:   T3:  [3..0]
  Columns 72..79:   T3:  [7..4]
  Columns 80..87:   T3:  [11..8]
  Columns 88..95:   T3:  [15..12]
  Columns 96..103:  T3:  [19..16]
  Columns 104..111: T3:  [23..20]
  Columns 112..119: T3:  [27..24]
  Columns 120..127: T3:  [31..28]

ROW 9:
  Columns 64..71:   T7:  [3..0]
  Columns 72..79:   T7:  [7..4]
  Columns 80..87:   T7:  [11..8]
  Columns 88..95:   T7:  [15..12]
  Columns 96..103:  T7:  [19..16]
  Columns 104..111: T7:  [23..20]
  Columns 112..119: T7:  [27..24]
  Columns 120..127: T7:  [31..28]

ROW 10:
  (cells are empty/blank in the figure — implied continuation of pattern)

ROWS 11..14: (indicated by "..." — blank/implied continuation)

ROW 15:
  Columns 64..71:   T31: [3..0]
  Columns 72..79:   T31: [7..4]
  Columns 80..87:   T31: [11..8]
  Columns 88..95:   T31: [15..12]
  Columns 96..103:  T31: [19..16]
  Columns 104..111: T31: [23..20]
  Columns 112..119: T31: [27..24]
  Columns 120..127: T31: [31..28]

==============================================================
4. THREAD NUMBERING PATTERN
==============================================================
Rows 0-7 (upper half of M=16):
  Row 0:  Thread T2
  Row 1:  Thread T6
  Row 2:  Thread T10 (implied by pattern)
  Row 3:  Thread T14 (implied)
  Row 4:  Thread T18 (implied)
  Row 5:  Thread T22 (implied)
  Row 6:  Thread T26 (implied)
  Row 7:  Thread T30

Thread stride in upper half: +4 per row (T2, T6, T10, T14, T18, T22, T26, T30)

Rows 8-15 (lower half of M=16):
  Row 8:  Thread T3
  Row 9:  Thread T7
  Row 10: Thread T11 (implied)
  Row 11: Thread T15 (implied)
  Row 12: Thread T19 (implied)
  Row 13: Thread T23 (implied)
  Row 14: Thread T27 (implied)
  Row 15: Thread T31

Thread stride in lower half: +4 per row (T3, T7, T11, T15, T19, T23, T27, T31)

Upper half uses even-offset threads starting at T2 (mod 4 = 2)
Lower half uses odd-offset threads starting at T3 (mod 4 = 3)

==============================================================
5. BIT FIELD ASSIGNMENTS WITHIN EACH THREAD'S 32-BIT REGISTER
==============================================================
Each thread holds a 32-bit metadata register. The bits are distributed across the 8 column groups as follows:

Column group 64..71:   bits [3..0]   (4 bits)
Column group 72..79:   bits [7..4]   (4 bits)
Column group 80..87:   bits [11..8]  (4 bits)
Column group 88..95:   bits [15..12] (4 bits)
Column group 96..103:  bits [19..16] (4 bits)
Column group 104..111: bits [23..20] (4 bits)
Column group 112..119: bits [27..24] (4 bits)
Column group 120..127: bits [31..28] (4 bits)

Total: 8 groups x 4 bits = 32 bits per thread register

Each 4-bit field covers 8 columns. Within each 8-column span, 2-bit sub-fields encode which 2 of 4 elements are non-zero (2:4 structured sparsity). Two 2-bit selectors per 4-bit field = 2 pairs of elements selected from 4.

==============================================================
6. METADATA ENCODING FORMAT
==============================================================
For .u4/.s4/.e2m1 types with k=128 and 2:4 sparsity:
- Every group of 4 consecutive elements along the K dimension has exactly 2 non-zero values
- Each 2-bit selector encodes which 2 of 4 elements are retained
- 4 bits per 8-column group encodes two consecutive 2:4 groups (columns i..i+3 and i+4..i+7)
- The metadata register for each thread is 32 bits wide, covering all 8 column groups (64 columns total for this half)
- Combined with the companion figure (columns 0-63), the full 32-bit metadata register covers all k=128 columns

==============================================================
7. DIAGONAL ARROW ANNOTATIONS
==============================================================
Two diagonal arrows are visible in the figure:
Arrow 1: Points from the area of row 2 / column 88..95 leftward-downward toward row 7 / column 72..79, indicating the continuation of the thread pattern (T10 through T30) fills rows 2-7.
Arrow 2: Points from the area of row 10 / column 104..111 rightward-upward toward row 10 / column 120..127, indicating the continuation of the thread pattern (T11 through T31) fills rows 10-15.
These arrows visually indicate that the blank rows follow the same thread-increment-by-4 pattern as the explicitly shown rows.

==============================================================
8. RELATIONSHIP TO FULL k=128 LAYOUT
==============================================================
This figure covers columns 64-127 (the second 64 columns of k=128).
The companion figure covers columns 0-63 (the first 64 columns).
Together, both figures define the complete 32-bit metadata register content for each thread.
Bits [31..0] of each thread's metadata register are split: bits for columns 0-63 come from the first figure, bits for columns 64-127 come from this figure, with this figure assigning bits [31..0

  >
  >
  > Figure 147 Sparse MMA .m16n8k128 metadata layout for columns 64–127 for `.u4`/`.s4`/`.e2m1` type.

##### 9.7.14.6.3. [Multiply-and-Accumulate Instruction: `mma.sp` / `mma.sp::ordered_metadata`](#warp-level-matrix-instructions-sparse-mma)

`mma.sp`, `mma.sp::ordered_metadata`

Perform matrix multiply-and-accumulate operation with sparse matrix A

Syntax

Half precision floating point type:

```
mma.spvariant.sync.aligned.m16n8k16.row.col.dtype.f16.f16.ctype  d, a, b, c, e, f;
mma.spvariant.sync.aligned.m16n8k32.row.col.dtype.f16.f16.ctype  d, a, b, c, e, f;

.ctype     = {.f16, .f32};
.dtype     = {.f16, .f32};
.spvariant = {.sp, .sp::ordered_metadata};
```

Alternate floating point type:

```
mma.spvariant.sync.aligned.m16n8k16.row.col.f32.bf16.bf16.f32     d, a, b, c, e, f;
mma.spvariant.sync.aligned.m16n8k32.row.col.f32.bf16.bf16.f32     d, a, b, c, e, f;
mma.spvariant.sync.aligned.m16n8k8.row.col.f32.tf32.tf32.f32      d, a, b, c, e, f;
mma.spvariant.sync.aligned.m16n8k16.row.col.f32.tf32.tf32.f32     d, a, b, c, e, f;
mma.spvariant.sync.aligned.m16n8k64.row.col.f32.f8type.f8type.f32 d, a, b, c, e, f;
mma.sp::ordered_metadata.sync.aligned.m16n8k64.row.col.kind.dtype.f8f6f4type.f8f6f4type.ctype d, a, b, c, e, f;

.f8type     = {.e4m3, .e5m2};
.spvariant  = {.sp, .sp::ordered_metadata};
.f8f6f4type = {.e4m3, .e5m2, .e3m2, .e2m3, .e2m1};
.kind       = {kind::f8f6f4};
.ctype      = {.f16, .f32};
.dtype      = {.f16, .f32};
```

Alternate floating point type with block scaling:

```
mma.spvariant.sync.aligned.m16n8k128.row.col.kind.block_scale{.scale_vec_size}.f32.e2m1.e2m1.f32.stype d, a, b, c, e, f, scale-a-data, {byte-id-a, thread-id-a}, scale-b-data, {byte-id-b, thread-id-b};

.spvariant      = {.sp::ordered_metadata};
.kind           = {.kind::mxf4};
.scale_vec_size = {.scale_vec::2X};
.stype          = {.ue8m0};

mma.spvariant.sync.aligned.m16n8k128.row.col.kind.block_scale.scale_vec_size.f32.e2m1.e2m1.f32.stype d, a, b, c, e, f, scale-a-data, {byte-id-a, thread-id-a}, scale-b-data, {byte-id-b, thread-id-b};

.spvariant      = {.sp::ordered_metadata};
.kind           = {.kind::mxf4nvf4};
.scale_vec_size = {.scale_vec::2X, .scale_vec::4X};
.stype          = {.ue8m0, .ue4m3};

mma.spvariant.sync.aligned.m16n8k64.row.col.kind.block_scale{.scale_vec_size}.f32.f8f6f4type.f8f6f4type.f32.stype d, a, b, c, e, f, scale-a-data, {byte-id-a, thread-id-a}, scale-b-data, {byte-id-b, thread-id-b};

.spvariant      = {.sp::ordered_metadata};
.kind           = {.kind::mxf8f6f4};
.scale_vec_size = {.scale_vec::1X};
.f8f6f4type     = {.e4m3, .e5m2, .e3m2, .e2m3, .e2m1};
.stype          = {.ue8m0};
```

Integer type:

```
mma.spvariant.sync.aligned.shape.row.col{.satfinite}.s32.atype.btype.s32 d, a, b, c, e, f;

.shape     = {.m16n8k32, .m16n8k64}
.atype     = {.u8, .s8};
.btype     = {.u8, .s8};
.spvariant = {.sp, .sp::ordered_metadata};

mma.spvariant.sync.aligned.shape.row.col{.satfinite}.s32.atype.btype.s32 d, a, b, c, e, f;

.shape     = {.m16n8k64, .m16n8k128}
.atype     = {.u4, .s4};
.btype     = {.u4, .s4};
.spvariant = {.sp, .sp::ordered_metadata};
```

Description

Perform a `MxNxK` matrix multiply and accumulate operation, `D = A*B+C`, where the A matrix is
`MxK`, the B matrix is `KxN`, and the C and D matrices are `MxN`.

A warp executing `mma.sp.sync/mma.sp::ordered_metadata.sync` instruction compute a single matrix
multiply and accumulate operation.

Qualifier `.block_scale` specifies that the matrices `A` and `B` are scaled with `scale_A`
and `scale_B` matrices respectively before performing the matrix multiply and accumulate operation
as specified in the section [Block Scaling for mma.sync](#warp-level-block-scaling). The data type corresponding
to each of the element within `scale_A` and `scale_B` matrices is specified by `.stype`.
Qualifier `.scale_vec_size` specifies the number of columns of `scale_A` matrix and number of
rows in the matrix `scale_B`.

The valid combinations of `.kind`, `.stype` and `.scale_vec_size` are described in
[Table 36](#mma-scaling-kind-type-valid-combination). For `mma` with `.kind::mxf4` when the
qualifier `.scale_vec_size` is not specified, then it defaults to `2X`. In contrast,
when `.kind` is specified as `.kind::mxf8f6f4` then the qualifier `.scale_vec_size`
defaults to `1X`. However, for `.kind::mxf4nvf4`, it is mandatory to provide valid
`.scale_vec_size`.

Operands `a` and `b` represent two multiplicand matrices A and B, while `c` and `d`
represent the accumulator and destination matrices, distributed across the threads in warp. Matrix A
is structured sparse as described in [Sparse matrix storage](#warp-level-sparse-matrix-storage) Operands `e` and `f` represent sparsity
metadata and sparsity selector respectively. Operand `e` is a 32-bit integer and operand `f` is
a 32-bit integer constant with values in the range 0..3.
When `.block_scale` qualifier is specified, operand `scale-a-data`, `scale-b-data` represents
the scale matrix metadata corresponding to `scale_A` and `scale_B` matrices respectively.
The tuple `{byte-id-a, thread-id-a}` and `{byte-id-b, thread-id-b}` represent selectors for
matrices `scale_A` and `scale_B` respectively from their corresponding metadata arguments
`scale-a-data`, `scale-b-data`. The operands `scale-a-data`, `scale-b-data` are of type
`.b32`. The operands `byte-id-a`, `thread-id-a`, `byte-id-b`, `thread-id-b` are unsigned
16-bit integer values. For more details on selector arguments refer
[Block Scaling for mma.sync](#warp-level-block-scaling) section.

Instruction `mma.sp::ordered_metadata` requires the indices in the sparsity metadata to be sorted
in an increasing order starting from LSB, otherwise behavior is undefined.

The registers in each thread hold a fragment of matrix as described in
[Matrix fragments for multiply-accumulate operation with sparse matrix A](#warp-level-matrix-fragments-for-sparse-mma).

The qualifiers `.dtype`, `.atype`, `.btype` and `.ctype` indicate the data-type of the
elements in the matrices D, A, B and C respectively. The qualifier `.stype` indicate the
data-type of the elements in the matrices `scale_A` and `scale_B`. In case of shapes
`.m16n8k16`, `.m16n8k32` and `.m16n8k64`, `.dtype` must be the same as `.ctype`.

When `.kind` is either of `.kind::mxf8f6f4` or `.kind::f8f6f4`, the individual 4-bit and
the 6-bit floating point type elements must be packed in an 8-bit container. The matrix element
of type `.e2m1` resides in central 4 bits of the 8-bit container with padding in the upper 2
bits and lower 2 bits of the container. When the matrix element is of type `.e3m2` or `.e2m3`,
the matrix element resides in the lower 6 bits of the 8-bit container with padding in the upper
2 bits of the container. In contrast, note that when using `mma` with `.kind::mxf4` or
`.kind::mxf4nvf4`, no explicit padding is necessary even though matrix elements are of type
`.e2m1`.

Precision and rounding :
:   * `.f16` floating point operations :

      Element-wise multiplication of matrix A and B is performed with at least single
      precision. When `.ctype` or `.dtype` is `.f32`, accumulation of the intermediate values
      is performed with at least single precision. When both `.ctype` and `.dtype` are specified
      as `.f16`, the accumulation is performed with at least half precision.

      The accumulation order, rounding and handling of subnormal inputs are unspecified.
    * `.e4m3`, `.e5m2`, `.e3m2`, `.e2m3`, `.e2m1` floating point operations :

      Element-wise multiplication of matrix A and B is performed with specified precision. Accumulation
      of the intermediate values is performed with at least single precision.

      The accumulation order, rounding, and handling of subnormal inputs are unspecified.
    * `.bf16` and `.tf32` floating point operations :
